headline: Why the second round of France's municipal elections matters
description: The vote on Sunday, March 22, will provide valuable national insights, with a focus on the far-right's result, the impact of mergers or divisions within the left and right and the right's gamble to stay in the race.

headline: US and Iran trade threats of expanding war after strikes near sites tied to nuclear programs
Trump settles scores with former prosecutor Robert Mueller following news of his death
India faces major gas shortage as Middle East conflict disrupts supply
description: With restrictions, a black market and illegal storage networks, India's vulnerability has been exposed by the conflict affecting the Strait of Hormuz, through which 90% of its liquefied petroleum gas transits.

headline: Cuba hit by second nationwide blackout in a week
description: Cuba's national power network failed for the second time in less than a week, leaving streets pitch black and forcing people to navigate using flashlights. Blackouts have intensified because of aging infrastructure and a US oil blockade.

headline: Charif Majdalani: Lebanon is 'truly being dragged against its will into new calamities'
description: 'Writers on the War in the Middle East' 2/4. Le Monde asked writers Taleb Alrefai, Charif Majdalani and Lila Azam Zanganeh for their perspectives on the war.

headline: Dnipro, the 'heart of Ukraine' where residents, refugees and military live side by side
description: This industrial city, considered a stronghold of the Jewish community, embodies the country's history and a certain idea of resistance to the advances of the Russian army, four years after the start of the war.

headline: El Niño's likely return sparks fears of worsening climate crisis
French prosecutors suspect Musk encouraged deepfakes controversy to inflate X value
Russia resumes use of space launch site damaged in accident
Pesticides, pharmaceuticals and industrial chemicals found everywhere in seawater
London exhibit highlights a meeting between two major suffragettes
Slovenia votes in tight polls, with conservatives eyeing comeback
Polluted drinking water takes center stage in Denmark's election campaign
Is anti-scooter sentiment fueled by class contempt?
The UAE reorganizes its arms supply network for Sudanese paramilitaries
The truffle epidemic: 'I'm worried we'll soon have truffle shampoo'
French bitcoin evangelical Eric Larchevêque wants his right-wing voice to be part of public debate
Chuck Norris, king of martial arts and Reagan-era action movies, has died
Despite exhaustion and fear of Iranian strikes, Israelis continue to support warring government
description: Nearly 80% of the population continues to support Benjamin Netanyahu's government after three weeks of conflict with Iran, fueled by the authorities' repeated announcements of victory.

headline: France's aircraft carrier located in real time by Le Monde through Strava data
description: As the Charles de Gaulle and its strike group approach the Middle East, Le Monde identified a French sailor using the Strava fitness application in the Mediterranean Sea. This security flaw remains unaddressed despite our previous revelations.

headline: An ultra-modern artist: 'Renoir and Love' and 'Renoir Drawings' at the Musée d'Orsay
description: Challenging his reputation as a sentimental artist, two exhibitions of paintings and drawings highlight Renoir's affinity for working-class subjects and the complexity of his compositions.

headline: Energy crises must accelerate the fight against climate change
'The war scripted by Trump is being tested by an Iranian regime determined to survive at any cost'
War in Iran: 'Europe said no to the US. This is new, and it is a major development'
